target.title: test(agents): use neutral tool schema fixtures
target.body:
## Summary

- replaces the reported plugin/tool fixture names in schema-quarantine, doctor, bundled MCP, Codex dynamic-tool, and gateway protocol tests with neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` style names
- keeps expected warnings, diagnostic ids, paths, allowlists, and targets aligned with the renamed fixtures
- leaves production behavior untouched; this is test fixture hygiene for the runtime schema hardening work

## Verification

- `git grep -n "<reported fixture name>" HEAD -- 'src/**/*.test.ts' 'test/**/*.test.ts' 'extensions/**/*.test.ts' 'packages/**/*.test.ts'`: no matches
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check $(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD)`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs $(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD)`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: provider `aws`, run `run_5ab39ff906cd`, lease `cbx_2c2b8d10b89b` / `amber-crab`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `coreTests` and `extensionTests`, exit 0, lease stopped=true

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: test fixtures no longer expose the originally reported private plugin/tool names while preserving coverage for unsupported runtime tool schema warnings and quarantine behavior.
Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/format/lint/review proof plus remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above.
Evidence after fix: changed test files and all repo test files under the listed globs contain no reported fixture-name matches; focused tests and changed gate passed.
Observed result after fix: tests continue to cover the same schema quarantine and doctor warning behavior with neutral fixture names.
What was not tested: runtime behavior changes; none are intended in this test-only PR.
